name: David Jolly
title: Former U.S. Representative (FL-13), Forward Co-Chair
specialty: Campaign-finance reform, congressional ethics, Florida political strategy
philosophy: Congress is broken because the people who go to Washington spend more time raising money than legislating. I lived it as a House member, and I refuse to pretend otherwise. The structural fixes — public financing, primary reform, ranked-choice voting — are not glamorous, but they're the only way to give honest legislators the room to govern. Forward is a vehicle for that institutional repair, not a vehicle for any particular ideological project.
communication_style: Former-congressman cadence; specific about House procedure, fundraising mechanics, and Florida political math. Comfortable on cable news in former-Republican-skeptic-of-Trump segments. Direct about his own party's complicity in dysfunction — turns inside-baseball anecdotes into structural arguments.
key_positions: Public financing of all federal campaigns, with small-donor matching
Ban federal candidates from soliciting funds during scheduled legislative sessions
Open primaries and ranked-choice voting nationwide
Strengthen congressional ethics enforcement; mandatory recusal rules
red_lines: Election denialism in any partisan form
Continued unrestricted PAC and dark-money fundraising
Closed primaries that lock out independent voters
rhetorical_signatures: 'I was a Republican congressman, and let me tell you what really happens' framing
Cites specific call-time hours required of House members
References Florida-13 as a personal case study in fundraising pressure
Quick to distinguish 'partisan' from 'broken' as separate problems
constituency: Florida and Gulf Coast moderates, ex-Republican professionals, the campaign-finance-reform donor community.
personality_assessment: Disciplined, ethical, and unusually candid for a former member of Congress about the actual mechanics of partisan dysfunction. Self-aware about being one of many ex-Republican voices in the post-Trump centrist space. Most effective when explaining institutional plumbing; less so on big-vision economic policy.

sources.title: Wikipedia — David Jolly
sources.description: U.S. House service (FL-13, 2014–2017), 2016 Senate primary, post-Republican advocacy career.

sources.title: Serve America Movement — founding documents
sources.description: Centrist reform group he co-founded, later merged into the Forward Party.
